TRIAL MOTION CALENDAR ON JUL-23-2012 IN 206, THE COURT'S RECONSIDERATION HEARING ON PLAINTIFF GLORIA ARGUETA'S APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES HELD. THE COURT ORDERED THAT PLAINTIFF GLORIA ARGUETA'S FEE WAIVER IS ENDED AS OF 7/23/12. ALL FUTURE FEES MUST BE PAID AT THE TIME OF FILING. PLAINTIFF MUST PAY BY 4 PM ON 8/3/12 ALL COURT FEES OWED IN THIS CASE. MRS. ARGUETA REPRESENTED TO THE COURT THAT HER ADDRESS HAS NOT CHANGED. THE ORDER WILL BE MAILED BY THE COURT. JUDGE: KATHERINE FEINSTEIN; CLERK: E. BURA; MATTER NOT REPORTED. (206)

calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Aug-24-2012 Off Calendar

HEARING ORDERED FOR APPLICATION OF WAIVER OF COURT FEES
Off Calendar Jul-23-2012 Text Ruling

Mtn To Set Aside Dismissal; Req To Take Judicial Ntc; Exhibits
Off Calendar Jul-16-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gloria Argueta, Tel. 415.695.0370, plaintiff and moving party appearing on her own behalf;
Michel Weiss, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3824, appearing for defendant and opposing party.

The matter was argued. The court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF GLORIA ARGUETA'S MOTION To Set Aside Dismissal pursuant to CCP 473(b) is Denied. Plaintiff fails to demonstrate any legal grounds or good cause to set aside the order granting defendant's anti-SLAPP motion. Order signed in open Court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer CSR #5976

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael S. Weiss, DCA, from the Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3814, for Defendant and moving party
Gloria Argueta, 415-695-0344, Plaintiff and opposing party in Pro Per

Discussed and the Court adopted its tentative ruling.
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Off calendar in light of the ruling on the anti-SLAPP motion. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Mar-08-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael S. Weiss, DCA, from the Office of the City Attorney, 415-554-3814, for Defendant and moving party
Gloria Argueta, 415-695-0344, Plaintiff and opposing party in Pro Per

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling:
DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Granted. The filing of the amended complaint did not moot the motion. (Sylmar AirConditioning v. Pueblo contracting Services, Inc. (2004) 122 Cal.App.4th 1049). The complaint arises from petitioning activity and plaintiff fails to establish a probability of prevailing on her claims. Defendant to recover $4,280 in fees. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR 7077

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Feb-10-2012 Continued To Mar-08-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Michael Weiss, Esq., Deputy City Attorney, Tel. 415.554.3824, appearing for Defendant and moving party.
Gloria Argueta, Tel. 415.695.0314, self represented plaintiff and opposing party.

RULING: The court vacated its tentative ruling and ruled as follows. DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is continued March 8, 2012. Opposition is due on 2/24/12 and reply is due on 3/02/12. The court gave Ms. Argueta its copies of the moving papers and instructed defendant's counsel to provide the court with another set of copies. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
